frameworks.name: rate-distortion theory
frameworks.one_line: Normative capacity-limited encoding that trades off rate against task-relevant distortion

frameworks.name: capacity-limited visual working memory
frameworks.one_line: VWM treated as a bounded channel whose allocation adapts to stimulus and task

frameworks.name: statistical and categorical learning
frameworks.one_line: Subjects learn stimulus distributions and category structure shaping memory

core_question: Do people reallocate limited visual working memory capacity based on stimulus statistics and task demands?
key_assumptions: VWM has a finite, bounded capacity
Capacity can be dynamically reallocated in a task-dependent manner
Rate-distortion theory provides a normative account of VWM allocation
Stimulus regularities (mean, variance) can be tracked by the observer
manipulations: Imposed distributions on a stimulus dimension (leaf widths of plants)
Used naturalistic but precisely controlled plant images
key_findings: Subjects adapted VWM performance to the assigned stimulus distribution
Memory errors reflected the statistical regularities of to-be-remembered items
interpretations: VWM capacity is strategically reallocated based on stimulus statistics and task requirements
A rate-distortion framework captures flexible, robust allocation under capacity limits
